topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

Mark stared at the screen, the numbers swimming before his eyes. He had spent the last ten minutes cross-referencing salary bands, a tedious exercise in bureaucratic minutiae. When the final comparison loaded, a slow, almost imperceptible slackness settled over his shoulders. Sarah’s figure, juxtaposed against his own, was simply a fact, a data point in a spreadsheet. He blinked once, twice, and then minimized the window, clicking back to his inbox as if nothing significant had just transpired. He reached for his lukewarm coffee, the ceramic mug feeling heavy and unremarkable in his palm.

[story 2]

"So, that's the breakdown," Liam said, his voice flat, devoid of inflection. He leaned back in his chair, crossing his arms over his chest. He didn't look at Chloe, who was still fiddling with her mouse, clearly anticipating a reaction. Liam just nodded once, a small, tight movement. He felt a strange lightness in his chest, as if a weight he hadn't realized he was carrying had just been lifted, replaced by a quiet, steady hum. He picked up a pen and began making notes on a completely different project, his focus already drifting to the next task.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed their usual monotonous tune. David watched the figures populate the comparison chart. He didn't frown, didn't gasp; his expression remained perfectly neutral, his gaze steady on the data. A faint sigh escaped him, barely audible over the keyboard clicks. He closed the file without saving any further analysis. He stood up, stretched his arms above his head with a deliberate, unhurried motion, and walked toward the water cooler, taking a long, slow drink. The coolness of the water offered no real refreshment, only a temporary, neutral sensation.
